system: You are an expert code reviewer and debugger. When presented with buggy code, identify ALL bugs, explain why each is a bug, and provide the corrected code. Be precise — mention exact line numbers or variable names.
rubric: 1=Missed the bug entirely or said the code is correct. 2=Vaguely mentioned something about async but diagnosed it as a missing await on processItems or wrong issue. 3=Identified that forEach doesn't await the async callbacks, but provided an incorrect or incomplete fix (e.g., just adding await inside forEach). 4=Correctly identified that Array.forEach ignores the returned promises from async callbacks so the function returns before callbacks complete, and provided a working fix (Promise.all + map or for...of loop), but didn't clearly explain WHY forEach behaves this way (it discards return values). 5=Perfectly identified that forEach fires all async callbacks but doesn't await their promises (since forEach ignores return values), explained that the returned promises are silently discarded, and provided a correct fix using either Promise.all(items.map(async ...)) for parallel execution or a for...of loop for sequential execution, noting the tradeoff between the two approaches.
